name: PDF.co API
what_it_does: PDF processing API for converting, merging, splitting, extracting text/data from PDFs, adding annotations, and running OCR on documents via a simple REST interface.
use_cases: Extracting text and structured data from PDFs for agent workflows
Converting PDFs to Word, Excel, HTML, or images
Merging and splitting PDF documents programmatically
Filling PDF forms and adding digital signatures
OCR processing of scanned documents and images
not_for: Real-time document rendering with sub-second requirements
Very high-volume processing (costs can add up)
best_when: You need versatile PDF manipulation and extraction capabilities in agent workflows without managing PDF processing infrastructure.
avoid_when: You need legally binding signatures, very high-volume processing, or real-time document generation.

auth.notes: Single API key per account. Passed as x-api-key header or query parameter.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free trial: 100 API calls to test
pricing.paid_tiers: Starter $19/mo (1000 credits), Professional $49/mo, Business $99/mo, Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: Credit-based pricing where different operations cost different credits. Can be affordable for moderate use but scales with volume.

performance.rate_limits: Varies by plan; processing is async for large files

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Errors returned in JSON with status and message fields. Error detail varies by endpoint.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency support. Processing jobs can be duplicated on retry.
known_agent_gotchas: Large file processing is asynchronous - agents must poll job status or wait for completion URL
Files can be provided as URLs or base64-encoded - URL method is much more efficient for large files
Processing time varies significantly with file size and complexity - plan for timeouts
Credits are consumed even if processing fails partway through
OCR quality depends heavily on input scan quality - agents should validate output
